title: Ojas Maxivision Eye Hospital launches new centre in Juhu, Mumbai
summary: Ojas Maxivision Super Speciality Eye Hospitals has further strengthened their presence in Mumbai with the launch of the new centre in Juhu, while becoming one of the first eye care […]
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Newly expanded network expected to serve over 1 Lakh patients annually

Ojas Maxivision Super Speciality Eye Hospitals has further strengthened their presence in Mumbai with the launch of the new centre in Juhu, while becoming one of the first eye care networks in the city to offer a comprehensive suite of next-generation vision correction and cataract technologies.

As part of this expansion, the hospital has introduced the combination of ELITA SILK and WaveLight Plus InnovEyes for the first time in Mumbai both under one brand (Bandra Centre), further reinforcing its position as a Centre of Refractive Excellence. Western India continues to be a key growth zone for Maxivision, contributing approximately 30-35% to overall revenues, with Mumbai accounting for nearly 30-35% of the West zone business. The network is targeting 20-25% CAGR growth across the West over the next five years.

At its Bandra Centre of Refractive Excellence, Ojas Maxivision has introduced, for the first time in Mumbai, the combination of ELITA SILK and WaveLight Plus InnovEyes under one roof. Together, these technologies provide surgeons with access to two of the world’s most advanced refractive surgery platforms, allowing them to select the procedure best suited to each patient’s corneal characteristics, visual requirements, and lifestyle expectations.

WaveLight Plus InnovEyes represents the next generation of personalised laser vision correction. The platform uses advanced artificial intelligence to create a highly detailed 3D digital twin of the patient’s eye and virtually simulate treatment outcomes before surgery.

The expansion further strengthens the collaboration between Ojas Eye Hospital and Maxivision Super Speciality Eye Hospitals, with Ojas Maxivision aiming to expand its network from 03 centres currently to 30 centres by FY31, serving over 3 lakh patients annually. By offering both technologies within a single Centre of Refractive Excellence, Ojas Maxivision enables surgeons to recommend the technology that is truly best suited to the individual patient, reinforcing the hospital’s commitment to personalised eye care.

Dr G S K Velu, Chairman and Managing Director, Maxivision Super Speciality Eye Hospitals mentioned, “Mumbai represents one of the most important growth markets for us in Western India, not only because of its scale, but because of the rising demand for high-precision, technology-driven eye care. At Maxivision, growth is not just about increasing our footprint, it is about creating centres of clinical excellence backed by advanced technology, experienced specialists, and standardized patient outcomes. We are also planning to add three more centres in Mumbai in near future to make our hold stronger in the market.”

Dr Niteen Dedhia, Managing Director, Ojas Maxivision Eye Hospitals said, “Eye care is moving towards greater precision, personalisation and predictability, and the combination of WaveLight Plus InnovEyes and ELITA SILK reflects that shift. These technologies allow us to offer more customised refractive treatment and digitally guided refractive procedure planning, enhancing both accuracy and patient outcomes. With the newly expanded network expected to serve over 1 lakh patients annually and support approximately 20,000 surgeries each year, this launch marks an important step in making advanced ophthalmic care more accessible to people across Mumbai.
